summary: The first_proof_1 benchmark of 10 research-level problems shows that 7 problems (q1, q2, q3, q7, q8, q9, q10) have reached a fully resolved single-proof state, while q4, q5, and q6 remain active with multiple open issues. q4 is the most contested with 22 open issues, q5 has 14 open, and q6 is nearly closed with just 1 open issue, indicating these three concentrate nearly all remaining verification effort.
problems: q4 has 22 open issues and 23 resolved, indicating a large, actively-disputed proof with unresolved limiting arguments, lemma justifications, and a dimensional error (JJ* vs J*J in the Hessian).

q5 has 14 open issues spanning a converse connectivity argument, well-definedness of chi^O(H) as a minimum, and a 'discrete slices' conclusion that does not follow — suggesting structural gaps in the argument.
q6 has only 1 open issue (a missing rank argument that the top-sigma bar... ) but a history of 15 resolved issues, indicating it is close to completion.
q4's open issues include a concrete technical error (dimensional mismatch in the Hessian) that may invalidate downstream steps until corrected.
All problems report 100 proof-status issues each, so resolution counts must be read against substantial total review volume.
highlights: 7 of 10 problems (q1, q2, q3, q7, q8, q9, q10) appear fully resolved with a single accepted proof each.
q6 is on the verge of completion with 15 resolved and only 1 remaining open issue.
q4, despite being the most contested, shows strong traction with 23 resolved issues, demonstrating active and productive review.
Open issues are concentrated in just three problems (q4, q5, q6), allowing focused allocation of reviewer effort.

suggested_todos.title: Resolve the single remaining rank argument in q6 to close it out
Fix the JJ* vs J*J dimensional error in the q4 Hessian and re-verify dependent lemmas
Triage q4's 22 open issues into blocking vs. cosmetic to identify a critical path
Address q5 well-definedness of chi^O(H) and the 'discrete slices' gap
Validate the q4 limiting argument from simple roots to general case
Spot-check the 7 resolved problems for regressions before final sign-off
